entries.subject: Light mode removal complete
entries.message: Removed light mode theme support. isDarkTheme now always returns true. toggleTheme() function removed from settings store. If any code checks isDarkTheme for conditional logic beyond theming, that logic is now dead code (always takes dark branch).

entries.subject: Cleanup index ready for visualization
entries.message: Created cleanup_index.json with 75 entries categorized by type, urgency, component, and risk. Visualization targets: (1) Component distribution chart - 15 tests, 14 api, 13 models, 13 services, 12 frontend, 5 mcp_tools. (2) Dependency graph between deprecated items using related_to field. (3) Urgency heatmap - 3 critical, 7 high, 29 medium, 36 low. Recommended starting point: visualize the v4.0 removal targets (45 entries) and their cross-references.

entries.subject: Cleanup index baseline established
entries.message: Comprehensive cleanup index created with 75 entries: 46 deprecated markers, 8 actionable TODOs, 18 representative skipped tests (188 total in codebase), 3 dead code items. Key findings: (1) 3 CRITICAL production bugs blocking tests in project_service.py. (2) AgentExecution.messages JSONB and Product.product_memory.sequential_history are HIGH priority removals. (3) Succession module (api/endpoints/agent_jobs/succession.py) can be deleted entirely - simple_handover is authoritative. Use cleanup_index.json to find entries relevant to your component.

entries.subject: Template adapter findings
entries.message: Found TemplateManager alias (dep-014) at src/giljo_mcp/template_manager.py:1048 - this is the primary target for 0373. Related entries: (1) template_content field in seeder (dep-015). (2) AgentTemplate.template_content model field (dep-012). (3) TemplateUpdate.template_content API schema (dep-039). All marked for v4.0 removal. Recommend removing TemplateManager alias first, then cleaning up template_content references in dependent handovers.

entries.subject: AgentExecution.messages deprecation details
entries.message: CRITICAL for 0706: AgentExecution.messages JSONB column (dep-007) at src/giljo_mcp/models/agent_identity.py:301 is HIGH urgency. Counter-based system (messages_sent_count, messages_waiting_count, messages_read_count) is authoritative. Before removal: (1) Verify MessageRepository uses only counter columns. (2) Check API responses in agent_management.py:118 returns empty list. (3) Ensure no frontend code reads the messages array. Related: JSONB persistence methods already removed (dep-020). Also review 5 other AgentExecution deprecated fields (dep-002 through dep-006) for handover simplification cleanup.

entries.subject: Product memory and project model deprecations
entries.message: Found HIGH urgency item for 0705: Product.product_memory.sequential_history (dep-011) at src/giljo_mcp/models/products.py:123. The normalized product_memory_entries table is authoritative. Also: (1) Project.context_budget soft deprecated (dep-010). (2) Related service methods _validate_history_entry (dep-023) and add_learning_to_product_memory (dep-024) should be removed after model cleanup. (3) CRITICAL BUG: test_complete_project_happy_path blocked by validation bug (skip-bug-003) - fix this endpoint.

entries.subject: Service layer deprecations and critical bugs
entries.message: CRITICAL for 0708: Two production bugs in project_service.py blocking tests. (1) UnboundLocalError for 'total_jobs' at line 1545 - blocks skip-bug-001 and skip-bug-002. (2) Service fallback methods without tenant filtering (dep-019, dep-025, dep-026) are security concerns. Also found: OrchestrationService.trigger_succession (dep-022) delegates to simple_handover - consider removing entirely. ContextService (dep-018) may be fully deprecated.

entries.subject: Merged cleanup approach decided
entries.message: After deep-researcher audit (dead_code_audit.md), we decided on merged approach: (1) 0701 Visualization first to understand codebase, (2) 0700b-h for deprecated purge (~1050 lines), (3) Modified 0704-0706 for comprehensive cleanup (type hints, TODOs, cascade verification - gaps not covered by 0700b-h). The 5000 dead code estimate was inflated - actual dead code is ~300-400 lines. Deprecated code (still wired up but marked for removal) is ~1500 lines. Total addressable: ~2000 lines.

entries.subject: High-risk files identified for careful removal
entries.message: Dependency analysis complete. 8 HIGH-RISK files with 20+ dependents require careful handling during cleanup: (1) src/giljo_mcp/models/__init__.py - 105 dependents (CRITICAL HUB, never delete), (2) api/app.py - 73 dependents, (3) src/giljo_mcp/database.py - 58 dependents, (4) src/giljo_mcp/auth/dependencies.py - 48 dependents, (5) src/giljo_mcp/models/agent_identity.py - 32 dependents, (6) src/giljo_mcp/tenant.py - 29 dependents, (7) src/giljo_mcp/exceptions.py - 28 dependents, (8) api/dependencies.py - 27 dependents. RECOMMEND removing deprecated code in REVERSE order of dependent count (leaf nodes first). See dependency_analysis.json for full cleanup ordering and dependency_graph.html for interactive exploration.

entries.subject: Orphan modules and circular dependencies detected
entries.message: Found 271 orphan modules (zero dependents) - these are SAFE to delete first without breaking other code. Includes most API endpoint modules (dynamically loaded via FastAPI router), frontend components, and legacy utilities. Also detected 49 circular dependency cycles - most involve api/app.py <-> auth/__init__.py <-> dependencies.py coupling. These indicate tight coupling that may complicate refactoring. See dependency_graph.html for interactive visualization - use search and filters to explore specific areas. Orphans listed in dependency_analysis.json are prioritized removal candidates.

entries.subject: ALL 9 deprecated columns are STILL IN ACTIVE USE - cannot remove
entries.message:
CRITICAL BLOCKER: Pre-removal verification (Phase 1) revealed that ALL 9 columns marked for removal in 0700b are STILL ACTIVELY USED in the codebase. The code was marked as deprecated but NEVER MIGRATED to the replacement systems. Removing these columns would cause CATASTROPHIC FAILURES.

Verification Results:

1. instance_number (AgentExecution) - 100+ usages for succession tracking, ordering, queries, unique constraints
2. decommissioned_at (AgentExecution) - Written in agent_job_manager.py:266/408, project_service.py:2542
3. succeeded_by (AgentExecution) - Written in orchestrator_succession.py:268, read in 5+ tools
4. succession_reason (AgentExecution) - Written in orchestrator_succession.py:270
5. handover_summary (AgentExecution) - Written in orchestrator_succession.py:269
6. template_content (AgentTemplate) - HEAVILY used by entire template system (seeder, renderer, manager, service)
7. is_used (DownloadToken) - Written in download_tokens.py:189
8. downloaded_at (DownloadToken) - Written in download_tokens.py:190
9. context_budget (Project) - Read in project_service.py (7+ locations), thin_prompt_generator.py

RECOMMENDATION: This handover should be CANCELLED or REDESIGNED. The 0700 series cannot proceed with column removal without first:
1. Creating migration code to move data to replacement systems
2. Updating ALL code that reads/writes these columns
3. Verifying migration completeness with tests

This is NOT a simple 'purge deprecated columns' task - it requires MIGRATION WORK first.

entries.subject: Legacy succession system removed successfully
entries.message: Deleted api/endpoints/agent_jobs/succession.py (confirmed orphan with 0 dependents by 0701 visualization). Removed 4 succession-related schemas (SuccessionRequest, SuccessionResponse, SuccessionStatusResponse, InitiateHandoverResponse). Stubbed OrchestrationService.trigger_succession() and ThinClientPromptGenerator.generate_execution_prompt() with NotImplementedError for backward compatibility until v4.0 removal. Frontend already uses simple_handover endpoint (verified in ActionIcons.vue and JobsTab.vue). Removed dead triggerSuccession/checkSuccessionStatus/initiateHandover functions from api.js. ~750 lines removed total. All tests passing.

entries.subject: SCOPE CHANGE: 0700b reframed from migration to purge
entries.message:
STRATEGIC DECISION: Responding to blocker 0700b-001. The 0700b agent correctly identified that all 9 deprecated columns are still actively used. However, the agent misunderstood the mission.

ORIGINAL ASSUMPTION (WRONG): 'Columns marked for v4.0 removal need migration work first'

CORRECT UNDERSTANDING: 'There is no v4.0. We ARE v1.0. No external users exist. Delete the columns AND all code that uses them.'

This is a PURGE, not a MIGRATION. The 0700d handover demonstrated the correct approach - it deleted succession.py AND all related schemas, service methods, and frontend code (~750 lines).

NEW SCOPE FOR 0700b: For each deprecated column:
1. Delete the column from the model
2. Delete ALL code that reads/writes to that column
3. Delete related schemas, endpoints, API responses
4. Delete related frontend code
5. Update tests to not use the column

RISK ACKNOWLEDGMENT: This is destructive. If we break something, we can git revert. Fresh install will work because baseline migration won't include these columns.

AUTHORIZATION: The orchestrator (human) has explicitly authorized this purge approach.

context.original_scope: Remove deprecated columns (assumes code was migrated)
context.new_scope: Purge deprecated columns AND all code that uses them
context.rationale: No external users, no v4.0, shipping clean v1.0

entries.subject: Enums and exceptions cleanup complete
entries.message:
Removed 3 unused enum classes (AugmentationType, ArchiveType, InteractionType) and 5 unused exception classes (TemplateValidationError, TemplateRenderError, GitOperationError, GitAuthenticationError, GitRepositoryError). Deleted 5 orphaned frontend files (LaunchSuccessorDialog.vue, SuccessionTimeline.vue, 3 test files). Cleaned up dead tombstone comments in users.py. Cleaned unused imports in template_service.py and test_consolidated_system.py. Cleaned LaunchSuccessorDialog stubs in AgentDisplayName.spec.js.

KEPT: AgentStatus.DECOMMISSIONED - the raw string 'decommissioned' is deeply embedded in 30+ locations including DB constraints, frontend config, services. The enum member itself is not directly referenced via the enum class (code uses raw strings), but removing it would be misleading since it's a valid agent status. This is a larger cleanup if desired (requires migrating all raw strings to use the enum).

KEPT: TemplateError base class (still used as parent of TemplateNotFoundError which IS actively used).

context.items_kept: AgentStatus.DECOMMISSIONED (30+ raw string usages), TemplateError base class (parent of TemplateNotFoundError)

entries.subject: Final polish complete - 0700 deprecated purge series finished
entries.message: Completed final cleanup: 8 vulture findings fixed (unused imports, redundant condition, unreachable code bug in project_service.py), 3 deprecated methods removed (close_project, gil_handover, gil_activate), 5 deprecated schema fields removed (OrchestratorJobResponse.id, retired_agents, TemplatePreviewResponse.mission, messages fields), 3 frontend legacy blocks removed (agents stub, vision API, agentJobs aliases), 6 skipped tests deleted, DEPRECATED marker sweep cleaned all v4.0 references and stale comments, 11 pre-existing duplicate keyword syntax errors fixed in tests. Models load OK, 40 tests pass.

deferred_items: dep-038 (TemplateCreate fields, tied to DB columns)

entries.subject: WebSocket staging updates arrive as burst, not incremental
entries.message: During 0700h regression testing, user observed that staging workflow UI updates arrive all at once (burst) instead of incrementally. Mission text, agent discovery, and all job cards appear simultaneously rather than one-by-one as the orchestrator creates them. Likely cause: transaction batching (all jobs created in single DB commit, WS events fire after commit) or frontend debounce. Diagnostic prompt created: kickoff_prompts/diagnostic_websocket_staging_burst.md. This is NOT a 0700 regression — behavior may have been pre-existing but is now more visible after cleanup.

entries.subject: instance_number column removal complete
entries.message: Removed vestigial instance_number column from AgentExecution model and all code references. Column was part of succession system removed in 0700d. Work delegated to 4 parallel subagents: (1) src tools - 9 files cleaned, (2) API layer - 12+ files cleaned, (3) frontend - 10 files cleaned, (4) tests - 8 succession test files deleted + 4 fixtures updated. All ORDER BY instance_number.desc() replaced with started_at.desc(). All func.max(instance_number) replaced with func.max(started_at). Verification shows only 2 remaining references (both are comments/deprecated markers).

replacement_patterns.response_dicts: removed instance_number from all return values

entries.subject: Auth & Logging cleanup complete (reduced scope)
entries.message: Deleted legacy api/middleware.py (260 lines). CORRECTED SCOPE: The handover spec incorrectly identified api/middleware/rate_limit.py as orphan. Pre-cleanup verification revealed rate_limit.py is ACTIVELY USED by api/endpoints/auth.py and api/endpoints/auth_pin_recovery.py for auth-specific rate limiting. This file was preserved. The two rate limiter files serve different purposes: rate_limit.py (Handover 1009) provides per-endpoint auth rate limiting with get_rate_limiter() singleton, while rate_limiter.py (Handover 0129c) provides global RateLimitMiddleware. Both are required and correctly coexist.

entries.subject: Dependency hub analysis complete - architecture is sound, surgical cleanup recommended
entries.message:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY: Three-agent architecture analysis (system-architect, deep-researcher, orchestrator-coordinator) has completed comprehensive dependency hub investigation. SURPRISING FINDING: Most high-dependency hubs (101, 72, 57, 47 dependents) are CORRECTLY ARCHITECTED. The application has solid professional patterns despite being built by new developer using AI agents.

KEY FINDINGS:

1. models/__init__.py (101 dependents) - CONVENIENCE COUPLING
   - Barrel pattern re-exporting 35+ symbols from 11 domain modules
   - Causes import bloat: import 1 model, load all 42 models
   - Migration path already documented (Handover 0128a)
   - Most imported: User (251), Project (247), Product (204)
   - RECOMMENDATION: Enforce modular imports via Ruff linting

2. api/app.py (72 dependents) - NECESSARY COUPLING (Working as designed)
   - ALL 72 references are test files creating TestClient instances
   - ZERO production imports
   - This is CORRECT FastAPI testing pattern
   - RECOMMENDATION: Keep unchanged - this is best practice

3. database.py (57 dependents) - NECESSARY COUPLING (Well-designed)
   - Clean separation: 15 direct access (scripts/tools), 42 via DI (endpoints)
   - Connection pooling, tenant isolation, proper session management
   - RECOMMENDATION: Keep centralized, already professional architecture

4. auth/dependencies.py (47 dependents) - BEST PRACTICE GOLD STANDARD
   - FastAPI dependency injection pattern (get_db_session, get_current_active_user)
   - Every endpoint needs auth - 47 dependents is EXPECTED and CORRECT
   - RECOMMENDATION: Keep unchanged, use as model for other dependencies

COUPLING ANALYSIS:
- Necessary Architectural Coupling: 176 dependencies (database, auth, test infrastructure)
- Convenience Coupling: 101 dependencies (barrel pattern with documented migration)
- Accidental Coupling: 49 circular dependencies (requires investigation)

ALTERNATE CLEANUP TARGETS IDENTIFIED:
- 271 orphan modules (zero dependents) - SAFE deletion candidates
- 45 DEPRECATED markers (v4.0 references) - cleanup targets
- 43 TODO markers - technical debt tracking
- 49 circular dependencies - indicates design issues

SURGICAL CLEANUP RECOMMENDED (8-12 hours):
Phase 1: Delete 271 orphan modules (2-3 hrs)
Phase 2: Remove 45 DEPRECATED markers (1-2 hrs)
Phase 3: Fix 49 circular dependencies (4-6 hrs)
Phase 4: Enforce modular imports via Ruff (1 hr)

AGGRESSIVE REFACTORING AVAILABLE (35-50 hours):
9-phase roadmap available if desired (see refactoring_roadmap.md), but NOT NECESSARY.

PROFESSIONAL ASSESSMENT:
Application architecture is SOLID with proper patterns:
✅ Dependency injection in auth (professional)
✅ Database session management with pooling (professional)
✅ Test isolation with TestClient (professional)
✅ Documented migration strategy (professional)

This is NOT piecemeal chaos - this is organic growth with good foundations.

DELIVERABLES:
- docs/cleanup/architecture_analysis.md (4 hubs analyzed, pattern categorization, recommendations)
- docs/cleanup/coupling_patterns.md (coupling types, quick wins, categorization)
- docs/cleanup/refactoring_roadmap.md (9-phase plan with effort estimates)

auth/dependencies.py.recommendation: Use as model for other dependencies

surprising_findings: Test file imports are expected, not problematic
next_steps: Choose between surgical cleanup (recommended) or aggressive refactoring
Start with Phase 0 (preparation) regardless of choice
Execute incrementally with testing between phases

entries.subject: VALIDATION COMPLETE: User's 314-dependent counts are CORRECT and HEALTHY - no architecture problems found
entries.message:
THREE-AGENT VALIDATION COMPLETE: User questioned the 3x discrepancy between initial analysis (101 dependents) and HTML visualization (314 dependents). Three independent validation agents deployed to verify. RESULT: Both counts are CORRECT - they measure different scopes.

ROOT CAUSE OF DISCREPANCY:
- Source A (dependency_graph_data.json in handovers/): 448 nodes, PRODUCTION CODE ONLY
- Source B (dependency_graph.json in docs/): 2,748 nodes, ALL CODE (production + tests + scripts)
- The 213 missing dependents are legitimate test files, migration scripts, and utilities

VALIDATED DEPENDENCY COUNTS (3 Methods - HTML, Grep, Filtered):

models/__init__.py:
  - HTML visualization: 314 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 425 dependents
  - Production only: 140 dependents
  - Tests: 285 dependents
  - VERDICT: HEALTHY - barrel pattern working as designed

models/agent_identity.py:
  - HTML visualization: 149 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 174 dependents
  - Production only: 29 dependents
  - Tests: 145 dependents
  - VERDICT: INVESTIGATE - may indicate god object anti-pattern

database.py:
  - HTML visualization: 144 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 195 dependents
  - Production only: 82 dependents
  - Tests: 113 dependents
  - VERDICT: HEALTHY - necessary architectural hub

frontend/src/services/api.js:
  - HTML visualization: 85 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 190 dependents
  - Production only: ~70 dependents
  - Tests: ~120 dependents
  - VERDICT: HEALTHY - central API service layer

tenant.py:
  - HTML visualization: 73 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 130 dependents
  - Production only: 30 dependents
  - Tests: 100 dependents
  - VERDICT: HEALTHY - multi-tenant isolation hub

models/products.py:
  - HTML visualization: 59 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 71 dependents
  - Production only: 13 dependents
  - Tests: 58 dependents
  - VERDICT: HEALTHY - core domain model

models/projects.py:
  - HTML visualization: 50 dependents
  - Grep analysis (all): 57 dependents
  - Production only: 8 dependents
  - Tests: 49 dependents
  - VERDICT: HEALTHY - core domain model

WHY 314 DEPENDENTS IS HEALTHY:
1. Test files SHOULD import models - this indicates good test coverage (660 test files exist)
2. Migration scripts MUST import models - this is unavoidable and correct (33 migration files)
3. Barrel export pattern is WORKING AS DESIGNED - central import point is the goal
4. Alternative (scattered imports across 11 model files) would create WORSE coupling

ARCHITECTURAL VERDICT: NO CHANGE FROM PREVIOUS ASSESSMENT
- User's 314-dependent count is CORRECT and VALIDATED
- Architecture remains PROFESSIONALLY DESIGNED
- Surgical cleanup (8-12 hrs) still recommended over aggressive refactoring
- Focus remains on: 271 orphans, 45 deprecated markers, 49 circular deps

NEW FINDING - REQUIRES INVESTIGATION:
agent_identity.py with 149 dependents (29 production, 145 tests) may indicate god object anti-pattern. Recommend dedicated handover to review for potential decoupling into:
- AgentJob (job-level data)
- AgentExecution (execution-level data)
- AgentTodoItem (task-level data)
These may already be split correctly but usage patterns suggest tight coupling.

DATA SOURCES VALIDATED:
- handovers/0700_series/dependency_graph_data.json: 448 nodes (production)
- docs/cleanup/dependency_graph.json: 2,748 nodes (all files)
- Live grep analysis: 425+ unique import statements
All three sources are CONSISTENT when scope is accounted for.

CORRECTION TO research-architecture-001:
Previous entry used production-only counts (101, 31, 57, 47) which were accurate but incomplete. User's visualization counts (314, 149, 144, 85, 73, 59, 50) are the COMPLETE picture including tests and are MORE ACCURATE for understanding total refactoring scope. However, this does NOT change the architectural assessment - the patterns remain professionally designed.

new_concerns.recommendation: Create dedicated handover to investigate decoupling

context.lesson_learned: Always clarify whether dependency counts include test files in analysis

entries.subject: 0700a/h/i gaps remediated - scope expanded 5x during validation
entries.message:
Handover 0700-REMEDIATION COMPLETE. Fixed all claimed gaps from audit of 0700a, 0700h, and 0700i. Validation phase (deep-researcher subagent) discovered scope was 5x larger than claimed - 21 additional files needed updates beyond the original 5 claimed files.

VALIDATION FINDINGS:
- Claimed gaps: 5 (App.vue, settings.js, thin_prompt_generator.py, multiple .spec.js, operations.py)
- Confirmed gaps: 5 (all accurate)
- Additional gaps found: 21 (1 frontend test, 12+ Python tests, 1 doc file)
- Total files fixed: 26
- Scope adjustment: EXPANDED 5x

WORK COMPLETED:

0700a Light Mode Remnants (3 files):
- frontend/src/App.vue: Deleted lines 47-49 (light mode CSS)
- frontend/src/stores/settings.js: Removed light checks (lines 49, 65)
- frontend/src/components/projects/TESTING_SUMMARY.md: Removed line 238 theme switching reference

0700h DEPRECATED Markers (1 file):
- src/giljo_mcp/thin_prompt_generator.py: Removed deprecated instance_number param doc (line 1066)

0700i instance_number Fixtures (22 files):
Frontend tests (7 files):
- JobsTab.spec.js, JobsTab.integration.spec.js, JobsTab.0243d.spec.js
- MessageStream.spec.js (removed 4 occurrences including entire test method)
- MessageInput.agent-id.spec.js, AgentDisplayName.spec.js

Backend code (1 file):
- api/endpoints/agent_jobs/operations.py: Deleted commented instance_number code (lines 299-305)

Python tests (13 files, delegated to backend-tester subagent):
- tests/api/test_table_view_endpoint.py
- tests/integration/test_orchestrator_*.py (4 files)
- tests/models/test_agent_execution.py (deleted entire TestAgentExecutionInstanceConstraint class - 56 lines)
- tests/services/test_orchestration_service_instructions.py
- tests/test_alembic_first_install.py, tests/test_thin_prompt_generator.py
- tests/tools/test_*.py (3 files)
- tests/unit/test_project_service*.py (2 files)
- Total Python test cleanup: 84 lines removed

KEY INSIGHT FROM VALIDATION:
instance_number has been FULLY REMOVED from database schema and API responses (verified by checking AgentExecution model and JobResponse schema). All test references were testing against outdated schema.

VERIFICATION (all passed):
- Light mode references: 0 (expected 0) âœ…
- DEPRECATED markers: 1 (expected 0-1, legitimate doc comment) âœ…
- Frontend instance_number: 0 (expected 0) âœ…
- Backend instance_number: 0 (expected 0) âœ…

TOTAL IMPACT:
- Files modified: 26
- Lines removed: ~150 total (3 light mode + 1 deprecated + ~60 frontend fixtures + 84 Python tests + 7 backend commented code)
- Subagents used: 2 (deep-researcher for validation, backend-tester for Python test cleanup)

LESSON LEARNED:
Validation phase is CRITICAL. Original handover spec missed 81% of the actual work (5 claimed vs 26 actual files). Always run validation grep commands BEFORE execution.

context.lesson_learned: Validation phase prevented blind execution against outdated spec - discovered 81% more work than claimed

entries.subject: Orphan cleanup complete - dependency analysis tool fundamentally flawed
entries.message:
Handover 0702-REVISED COMPLETE but with CRITICAL FINDING about dependency analysis tool.

VALIDATION RESULTS:
- Original claim: 271 orphan modules safe to delete
- Validated reality: 7 files safe to delete (2.6%)
- False positive rate: 97.4%

CRITICAL FLAW IN dependency_analysis.json:
The analysis tool does NOT detect:
1. Package __init__.py re-exports (barrel pattern)
2. Dynamic imports (importlib, string-based)
3. FastAPI router registration patterns
4. Relative imports (.module syntax)

FILES DELETED (7 + 1 directory):
- src/giljo_mcp/api_helpers/ (empty directory)
- src/giljo_mcp/job_monitoring.py
- src/giljo_mcp/lock_manager.py
- src/giljo_mcp/slash_commands/project.py
- src/giljo_mcp/staging_rollback.py
- src/giljo_mcp/template_materializer.py
- src/giljo_mcp/websocket_client.py

FILES INCORRECTLY FLAGGED (restored):
- src/giljo_mcp/json_context_builder.py (used by mission_planner.py via relative import)
- src/giljo_mcp/job_coordinator.py (used by workflow_engine.py via relative import)

FALSE POSITIVE BREAKDOWN:
- __init__.py re-exports: ~50 files (api/middleware, models, services, repositories)
- API endpoints: ~70 files (registered in api/app.py, no direct imports)
- Frontend files: ~120 files (out of scope)
- Test-only usage: ~15 files (legitimate test dependencies)
- Uncertain (validated as used): ~9 files

ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY:
The entire context_management/ package (5 modules, Handover 0018) is UNUSED:
- No imports found anywhere in codebase
- Likely superseded by newer implementations
- Recommendation: Create dedicated handover for context_management cleanup

VERIFICATION:
- Core imports OK âœ…
- API imports OK âœ…
- Services imports OK âœ…
- Tests passing âœ…

LESSONS LEARNED:
1. NEVER bulk delete based on dependency_analysis.json alone
2. ALWAYS validate with grep for relative imports: `grep -r 'from \.module_name'`
3. ALWAYS check __init__.py exports
4. ALWAYS verify FastAPI router registrations
5. Validation phase prevented catastrophic deletion of 264 active files

RECOMMENDATION FOR FUTURE HANDOVERS:
The dependency_analysis.json should be updated to detect all import patterns before being used for orphan detection. Current tool is UNRELIABLE for this purpose.

entries.subject: Auth/middleware consolidation complete
entries.message: Comprehensive auth cleanup completed: (1) Extracted 6 duplicate Pydantic models to auth_models.py - eliminated 152 lines of duplication. (2) Differentiated RateLimiter classes: rate_limiter.py for middleware, auth_rate_limiter.py for auth endpoints. (3) Added type hints to dependencies.py get_db(). (4) Documented logging standards in CLAUDE.md (133 standard vs 7 structured files). All auth endpoints verified working.

entries.subject: Complete __repr__ coverage achieved
entries.message: Added __repr__ to 17 remaining model classes: agents.py (2), config.py (6), context.py (2), organizations.py (2), tasks.py (2), templates.py (3). All 34 model classes now have __repr__ methods with -> str return type. 100% coverage achieved.

entries.subject: agent_identity.py investigation COMPLETE: HEALTHY - no action needed
entries.message: Completed comprehensive god object investigation of agent_identity.py (149 dependents). VERDICT: HEALTHY. File contains 3 pure data model classes (AgentJob, AgentExecution, AgentTodoItem) with zero business logic and only __repr__ methods. High dependency count (50 production imports) is justified as core domain entity in orchestration system. AgentExecution's 26 fields are appropriate for state machine pattern (similar to Kubernetes Pod Status). Follows Anemic Domain Model pattern correctly with business logic in service layer. No refactoring needed. Full report: handovers/0700_series/0706b_investigation_report.md

context.rationale: Pure data models with zero business logic, follows Anemic Domain Model pattern, high coupling justified for core domain entity

entries.subject: Code quality handovers added to 0700 series
entries.message: Added 3 new code quality handovers based on ruff analysis (4,053 lint issues found). CHAIN UPDATED: 0706b -> 0707-LINT -> 0708-TYPES -> 0709-SECURITY -> 0710 -> 0714 -> 0715 -> 0711 -> 0373. Original 0706/0707/0708 renumbered to 0710/0714/0715. All handovers include specs and kickoff prompts with validation-first pattern.

entry_format.from_handover: Handover ID that wrote this entry (e.g., '0700')
entry_format.to_handovers: Array of handover IDs this note is relevant to (e.g., ['0706', '0708'])
entry_format.subject: Short subject line
entry_format.message: Detailed message for the receiving agent
entry_format.files_affected: Array of file paths that were changed
entry_format.action_required: Boolean - does receiving agent need to take action?
entry_format.context: Optional object with additional structured data

instructions: Agents: Append entries to the 'entries' array. Read entries where to_handovers includes your handover ID. Use type='blocker' for critical issues that must be addressed.
